name: gmail
description: Reads Gmail messages, threads, drafts, labels, history, filters, send-as aliases, delegates, forwarding addresses, and mailbox profile, and writes approved reverse-ETL mutations (send/insert/import/modify/trash/delete messages and threads; draft and label lifecycle; filter, send-as, delegate, and forwarding-address management; vacation/language/IMAP/POP/auto-forwarding settings) via the Google OAuth 2.0 refresh-token grant.

risk.read: external Gmail API read of message/thread/draft/label/history/filter/send-as/delegate/forwarding-address/profile metadata
risk.write: external Gmail API mutation, including sending real outbound email, permanently deleting messages/threads/drafts, granting mailbox delegation, and changing account-wide forwarding/vacation/IMAP/POP settings
risk.approval: reverse ETL plan approval required before writes; several actions (send_message, send_draft, delete_message, delete_thread, delete_draft, create_delegate, update_auto_forwarding) warrant elevated operator scrutiny -- see docs.md Write actions & risks

conformance.reason: custom-auth-only (mode:custom AuthHook, no when-gated non-custom fallback); hook-covered, proven live by internal/connectors/paritytest/gmail (the AuthHook's real OAuth2 refresh-grant path) -- conformance's synthetic config has no real token_url and cannot exercise mode:custom auth at all, so every auth-resolving dynamic check would otherwise fail identically and uninformatively
